sentence_b: Pat sliced the carrots into the salad.
explanation: Same verb; transitive encodes simple action on a Patient with no specified endpoint; caused-motion adds a Goal PP, encoding the Theme's change of location as constructionally entailed.

sentence_b: Pat sliced Chris a piece of pie.
explanation: Same verb; ditransitive adds a Recipient and encodes intended transfer; transitive encodes only the action on the object with no transfer meaning.
Same verb; resultative adds a result XP (open) encoding the end-state of the object, absent from the plain transitive.

sentence_a: Pat sliced the carrots into the salad.
explanation: Same verb; resultative can be seen as a metaphorical extension of the caused-motion construction since the result phrase is interpreted as a metaphorical type of Goal; conceptual linking metaphor: CHANGE IS MOTION (Goldberg 1995: 115).

sentence_a: Pat sliced Chris a piece of pie.
sentence_b: Pat sliced the carrots into the salad.
explanation: Same verb; ditransitive encodes transfer to a Recipient; caused-motion encodes directed motion to a location, not possession change.
